examples: Reinfection will probably be less common in cases where the new antibiotic is prescribed than in cases where either penicillin or erythromycin is prescribed.
Since many patients stop taking those drugs within three days, reinfection is common in cases where these drugs are prescribed.
A strong immunity to reinfection develops after one year.
The most likely explanations are that the immune system is unable to clear the infection completely or that the site is peculiarly susceptible to reinfection, causing more damage each time.
